title: Nights
description: Portraits and landscapes shot in the last hour of daylight.
aiPrompt: Generate a Bret Easton Ellis style short text inspired by the picture it is attached to. Max characters 280. Also suggest a song (artist and title) that fits the mood of both the photo and the text you wrote.

photos.caption: The sun is doing that thing again where it makes everything look like a memory you haven't had yet. I'm driving nowhere on Mulholland and the light is so golden it feels like a lie. Someone texted me. I didn't look. The palm trees don't care. Neither do I.

She stood at the window and the light caught her like a Polaroid fading in reverse. I wanted to say something about how beautiful it was but the words felt expensive and I was already overdrawn. The golden hour doesn't forgive. It just exposes.

Everything is amber and warm and it means nothing. The city below is gilded in something that looks like hope but is really just refracted light and particulate matter. Someone at the party said 'magic hour.' I finished my drink and didn't correct them.
photos.songSuggestion: The War on Drugs - Under the Pressure

photos.caption: The light was the color of champagne nobody ordered. We sat there not talking, which was the most honest thing we'd done all week. Shadows getting longer. The playlist ended twenty minutes ago and no one noticed. That said everything.

Golden hour in a city built on disappearances. The light forgives every surface, every face, every lie told poolside. I put on sunglasses not because of the glare but because someone might see that I was feeling something. Unacceptable.

The sun dissolves into the horizon like a Klonopin under the tongue. Slow. Warm. Then gone. I'm standing somewhere expensive watching it happen and thinking about someone who stopped returning my calls in November. The sky doesn't know. The sky is perfect.

title: Nocturnal Animals
description: Long-exposure urban photography shot between midnight and 4am.
aiPrompt: Generate a Bret Easton Ellis style short text inspired by the picture it is attached to. Max characters 280. Also suggest a song (artist and title) that fits the mood of both the photo and the text you wrote.

photos.caption: The boulevard stretches out like a promise nobody intends to keep. Neon bleeds into wet asphalt. I'm driving someone else's car, listening to someone else's voicemail, feeling absolutely nothing. The city doesn't care. It never did.
Palm trees silhouetted against a sky the color of bruised skin. Someone texted me the address of a party in the hills. I deleted it. Then I drove there anyway. The gate was open. It's always open.
Streetlights hum at a frequency only the lonely can hear. I ordered another drink I won't finish. The bartender knows my name but not the real one. Nobody here uses real names. That's the point.

Downtown at 2 AM looks like a movie about downtown at 2 AM. Everything referencing everything. A girl in a white dress crosses against the light. I think about following her. I don't. I just watch the signal change.
The skyline is a flatline. Somebody OD'd at the Chateau again and nobody's talking about it. The pool is lit from underneath, turning faces into masks. I recognize everyone. I know no one.
Buildings like tombstones against indigo nothing. My reflection in every window, a stranger I keep running into. She said meet me on the roof. The elevator takes forever. I count floors like regrets.
photos.songSuggestion: Nine Inch Nails - A Warm Place

photos.caption: The freeway overpass frames the city like a photograph I'd never take. Red taillights dissolve into fog. My phone dies. For thirty seconds I feel something close to relief. Then the panic returns, quiet and familiar.

Everything glows but nothing illuminates. I park the car and sit there, engine running, AC on, staring at a skyline that looks like a screensaver. Somewhere in there someone is thinking about me. Probably not. The radio plays static beautifully.
photos.songSuggestion: Tangerine Dream - Love on a Real Train
